danger_signals.visual_form: explosion with horizontal and vertical flame spread across grid
danger_signals.meaning: immediate lethal hazard; player must evacuate blast radius

danger_signals.visual_form: themed enemy sprites on grid (varies by world)
danger_signals.meaning: hostile entities that deal damage on contact; must be eliminated or avoided

danger_signals.visual_form: breakable wall blocks filling grid spaces
danger_signals.meaning: obstacles blocking passage; destruction reveals items and paths but requires bomb use

danger_signals.visual_form: cascading flame-to-bomb ignition across multiple blocks
danger_signals.meaning: unpredictable area damage; spatial hazard from player's own bomb placement

danger_signals.visual_form: walls dropping from screen edges after 90 seconds in battle
danger_signals.meaning: playable area shrinkage; instant death on contact; time pressure escalation

danger_signals.visual_form: horizontal/vertical projectile lines from bomb center
danger_signals.meaning: collision with flame causes injury or death unless invincible

safety_signals.visual_form: open floor tile without walls or enemies
safety_signals.meaning: safe movement corridor; allows player mobility and evasion

safety_signals.visual_form: permanent wall blocks on grid (vs. soft walls)
safety_signals.meaning: protective cover; blocks bomb flames and enemy movement

safety_signals.visual_form: icons on screen revealed after wall destruction or enemy defeat
safety_signals.meaning: power-ups that enhance player capabilities (firepower, speed, bomb count, invincibility)

safety_signals.visual_form: temporary protection status (visual indicator on player)
safety_signals.meaning: safe from flame/bomb damage; window for aggressive tactics

safety_signals.visual_form: hidden door sprite revealed under destructible wall
safety_signals.meaning: level completion point; safe progression when all enemies cleared

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robot Bombermen in World 5 (mechanical variants of player character)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Diamond City setting (far north of Peace Town); themes vary by world

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Carat Diamond and Dr. Mook represent villain faction
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Themed enemy types per world; Robot Combat units designed for offensive capability
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Diamond City location; six themed worlds with distinct enemy sets and aesthetic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Progressive robot enemy sets in arena-based combat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: World 5 arena environment (no destructible walls or exit doors; continuous battle format)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Grid partially filled with soft walls; enemies positioned; exit door hidden beneath destructible block
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must clear enemies and locate exit to progress; cooperative two-player mode possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Twelve themed stages with different layouts; player trophy counter displays; round timer visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All bomb blasts instantly fatal; 1-4 players compete; fast-paced music reflects escalated tension

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hurry Up message displayed; indestructible walls begin dropping from screen edges (clockwise from bottom-left)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Playable area shrinks progressively; player must adapt to compressed space; contact with falling walls = instant death

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: No destructible walls or exit doors visible; arena environment; robot enemy waves enter progressively
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous battle format; player defeats one robot set, next set walks in automatically; different progression model than other worlds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Power-up icon appears on grid where wall or enemy was destroyed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can modify tactics; firepower/speed/bomb-count increases alter gameplay pacing; some items lost on death (Normal Game)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: World progression reaches stage 8 per themed world
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss enemy encounter replaces standard enemy clearance mechanic

environmental_storytelling.example: Six distinct worlds each with own enemy set and visual aesthetic; communicates escalating challenge and narrative scope (from Peace Town to Diamond City)
Removal of soft walls and exit door in World 5 signals transition from exploration/puzzle to pure combat; visual absence of progression mechanic reinforces continuous battle state

environmental_storytelling.visual_storytelling: Falling indestructible walls physically reduce playable space; visual manifestation of time pressure and escalating danger without dialogue

environmental_storytelling.example: Mechanical variants of Bomberman character visually communicate the villain's plot (stealing Bomberman's combat capabilities); robot design mirrors protagonist but signals hostile intent
Different item visual designs (firepower, speed, bomb count) communicate their mechanical effects; ice cream/pancakes/apples visually distinct from gameplay-altering power-ups

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Grid-restricted horizontal/vertical movement; can only move to adjacent squares
Drops bomb at feet; must evacuate before detonation; bomb pulses for several seconds before explosion
Visible countdown state; telegraphs detonation; allows player reaction time
Shoots flames horizontally and vertically; ignites adjacent bombs (chain reaction); instantly fatal to character contact
Themed enemies per world; roam grid; inflict damage on contact; eliminated by bomb blast
Next robot set automatically walks into arena; continuous battle cycle without level completion mechanic
Immune to flame and bomb damage; enables aggressive positioning
Drops from screen edges after 90 seconds; movement direction clockwise from bottom-left; instant kill on contact

notes: Super Bomberman communicates world-state primarily through grid-based environmental design and mechanic shifts rather than narrative UI. The transition from Normal Game (with soft walls, exits, enemy variety) to World 5 (arena, no progression mechanics, robot variants) and Battle Mode (instant-death, wall compression, trophy system) demonstrates state changes through visual and rule modifications. Faction identity is minimal but established through setting (Diamond City villains vs. Bomberman protagonist) and World 5's robot aesthetic. Danger signals emphasize spatial hazards (flames, bombs, walls) over narrative threats. Safety relies on empty space, cover, and power-ups. T2 tier: qualitative descriptors used; exact sprite dimensions/pixel values not documented in source.
